description: This control checks whether Amazon Elastic Block Store (EBS) encryption is enabled by default. The control fails if EBS default encryption is not enabled.

execution_guidance.blast_radius_summary: Affects account-wide security posture for account 696505809372.
execution_guidance.expected_outcome: Direct fix applies the change in AWS immediately: All new EBS volumes in this region will be encrypted by default. Security Hub should reflect the remediated state within 12-24 hours.
execution_guidance.label: Enable default EBS encryption (AWS managed key)

post_checks.message: Confirm the remediation run completed successfully and capture the run logs or audit artifacts for change evidence.
Verify the completed change only affected the intended account scope for AWS::::Account:696505809372.
Trigger immediate re-evaluation after the change lands and confirm the action closes without reopening.

pre_checks.message: Confirm this change is intended for AWS::::Account:696505809372 in account 696505809372 (us-east-1).
Direct fix mutates AWS state immediately after approval. Confirm change window and rollback owner before execution.
WriteRole is required for direct-fix execution.
Default encryption applies to newly created volumes; existing volumes are unchanged.

notes: Capture the current state before approval when your operating policy requires a backout snapshot.
rollback.summary: Use this rollback command to revert the live AWS change if verification fails or the blast radius is broader than expected.

title: EBS default encryption should be enabled
what_is_wrong: This control checks whether Amazon Elastic Block Store (EBS) encryption is enabled by default. The control fails if EBS default encryption is not enabled.
what_the_fix_does: Enables EBS default encryption for newly created volumes.
